name: clawcompiler-bootstrap
description: Bootstrap deterministic Regulatory Evidence Compiler contracts (schemas + conformance) before runtime service implementation.

userStories.title: Deterministic compiled evidence report schema
userStories.description: As a compliance pipeline, I need a deterministic compiled evidence report schema so control outcomes are machine-verifiable and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add compiled_evidence_report.v1 schema under packages/schema/compliance
Schema defines deterministic control-result status and reason-code fields
Schema remains additive and does not break compliance_report.v1.json
userStories.notes: Implemented schema contract with deterministic control status enums, reason-code formatting constraints, evidence hash references, and waiver->PARTIAL guardrail.

userStories.title: Signed compiled evidence report envelope schema
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need compiled reports wrapped in a signed envelope so payload hash and signer identity are cryptographically bindable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add compiled_evidence_report_envelope.v1 schema under packages/schema/compliance
Envelope enforces payload hash/signature/signer/issued_at fields
Envelope payload references compiled_evidence_report.v1
userStories.notes: Implemented envelope schema with deterministic hash/signature/signer constraints and explicit payload linkage to compiled_evidence_report.v1.

userStories.title: Dual-plane narrative schema membrane
userStories.description: As governance, I need narrative explanations to be optional and explicitly non-authoritative so deterministic compliance results cannot be overridden by prose.
acceptanceCriteria: Add compiled_evidence_narrative.v1 schema under packages/schema/compliance
Schema enforces authoritative:false
Schema enforces a fixed disclaimer string
Add conformance fixtures covering valid/invalid disclaimer and authoritative flag
userStories.notes: Implemented dual-plane narrative schema with hard membrane via const authoritative:false and fixed disclaimer, plus deterministic conformance vectors.

userStories.title: Canonical binary semantic evidence schema contract
userStories.description: As a compiler/verifier pipeline, I need a canonical binary semantic evidence schema contract so policy decisions are deterministic and machine-verifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: Define canonical schema family names and IDs for binary semantic evidence payload + envelope (single naming convention, no alias drift)
Use strict enums and additionalProperties:false at all decision-boundary objects
Additive-only integration into proof bundle contracts (optional field; no breaking required-field changes)
Reason-code registry for binary semantic evidence is explicitly enumerated and versioned
userStories.notes: R45 canonical intake source: docs/roadmaps/clawcompiler/oracle/2026-02-17/R45_BINARY_SEM_CANONICAL.md. S run contributes policy ordering only; malformed schema/code literals are excluded.

userStories.title: Deterministic runtime policy state machine for binary semantic evidence
userStories.description: As a verifier, I need a deterministic precedence state machine so binary semantic evidence yields reproducible fail-closed outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement precedence ordering exactly: crypto/causal failure -> contradiction -> analysis exhaustion -> inapplicable bounds -> partial -> valid
Enforce contradiction invariant: static ABSENT claim contradicted by verified dynamic PRESENT evidence yields deterministic fail-closed invalid outcome
No probabilistic or stringly-typed security decisions in policy evaluation
Deterministic verdict + reason-code outputs are stable for identical inputs
userStories.notes: Policy ordering and contradiction framing derived from R45 synthesis (A/C base + S policy donor).

userStories.title: Conformance fixtures for precedence and adversarial normalization
userStories.description: As an operator, I need conformance fixtures that lock policy semantics so regression or drift is caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fixture corpus that covers each precedence branch and expected deterministic reason code
Include normalized adversarial cases: chain forgery, capability arbitrage, analysis exhaustion, unsupported/inapplicable bounds, stripped/partial paths
CI conformance runner must assert exact verdict + reason-code determinism
Fixtures and runner are scoped to this domain and do not depend on narrative interpretation
userStories.notes: Derived from normalized R45 adversarial matrix in canonical synthesis doc.

metadata.status: bootstrap complete (CEC-US-001..003); R45 intake captured; CEC-US-004..006 planned
